title: Phase 1.2: Morning brief accountability tweak
description: Add to morning-brief.md prompt: check if yesterday's auto-draft was edited/published. If still status:skeleton, note 'yesterday's draft unpublished' in today's brief. Gentle accountability, not nagging.

title: Phase 2.2: X thread posting logic
description: Within /publish command: split essay into X thread format. First tweet = hook + Substack link. Reply chain = 2-4 key points. Use x-intel post_tweet MCP tool. Store tweet IDs in content/distribution/<slug>/x-thread-ids.json.

title: Phase 2.1: /publish slash command
description: New slash command ~/lakshminp.com/.claude/commands/publish.md. One invocation fans out a finished essay: (1) generate all platform versions via existing /distribute logic, (2) post to Medium via scripts/medium-post.js, (3) post X thread via x-intel post_tweet, (4) generate LinkedIn post for manual copy-paste, (5) prepare Reddit angles via ThreadHQ analyze_reddit_thread + get_subreddit_suggestions, (6) update publish-log.md. Substack stays manual until StackSweller ships.

title: Phase 4.2: Morning brief feedback loop integration
description: Modify morning-brief.md prompt: before ranking ideas, read most recent weekly review from memory. Apply playbook biases — if deploy essays got 3x engagement, boost deploy ideas. If ideas piling up, suggest fewer new ideas and push draft completion.

title: VM deployment: Docker image + compose
description: Dockerfile for claudeclaw: multi-stage Bun build, non-root user, Playwright chromium deps for browser MCP. Docker Compose with optional Ollama sidecar for local embeddings. Volume mounts for workspace/settings/memory persistence. Environment-based config (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CLAUDECLAW_MODEL, heartbeat interval). Main blocker: Claude CLI subscription auth is interactive — document workaround (SSH in, run claude once to auth, session persists in volume).

title: Pre-reset memory flush before session context reset
description: When 'Prompt is too long' is detected, claudeclaw currently just resets the session and retries fresh. This loses all in-progress context. We should add a pre-reset memory flush: before resetting, run a special Claude invocation that prompts the agent to write key context/state to memory files (memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md). Pattern from openclaw: runs a compact prompt instructing the agent to persist durable memories, then resets and starts fresh. Implementation: in runner.ts, in the 'Prompt is too long' handler, before resetSession(), run a special execClaude call with a memory flush prompt. The prompt should instruct the agent to write current task state, decisions, and key context to disk.
close_reason: Implemented pre-reset memory flush in runner.ts. Matches openclaw's pre-compaction memory flush pattern.

title: WhatsApp adapter via whatsapp-web.js
description: Add WhatsApp integration for personal use using whatsapp-web.js (unofficial Puppeteer-based client). Config key: settings.json `whatsapp: { enabled, allowedNumbers }`. Should mirror the Telegram adapter architecture. Note: requires Chrome/Chromium; QR code pairing on first run.

title: Phase 4.1: Weekly review job
description: New job ~/.claudeclaw/agents/business/jobs/weekly-review.md. Runs Sundays 10am. (1) X performance via x-intel get_my_recent_tweets — top/bottom 3 by engagement. (2) Reddit engagement from memory — which comments landed. (3) Pipeline status — essays published vs drafts unused. (4) Cross-platform patterns. Output: wins, misses, next week's playbook (topic/format/platform biases), pipeline health. Saved to memory for morning brief reference.

title: VM deployment: MCP connection health + auto-reconnect
description: MCP servers are stdio-based (spawned per claude -p invocation), so they don't expire. What does expire: (1) Slack tokens — add health check to heartbeat that tests Slack connection, alerts on failure. (2) X cookies — add periodic cookie validation (try a lightweight API call, if 401/403 alert to Slack). (3) Claude CLI auth session — detect auth expiry from runner stderr, alert immediately. (4) OpenAI embeddings API key — test on startup. Add a general MCP health probe that runs every 6 hours via cron job.

title: Semantic embeddings for memory_search
description: Add OpenAI/Ollama embedding support to memory-server.ts. SQLite chunk cache (bun:sqlite), hybrid cosine+keyword scoring, fallback to keyword when provider=none. Add memory.embeddings config section.

title: Slack adapter
description: Add Slack Socket Mode bot integration. Same message→runner→reply flow as the existing Telegram adapter. Socket Mode requires no public URL — just a bot token and app-level token. Implementation should mirror src/commands/telegram.ts. Config key: settings.json `slack: { botToken, appToken, allowedChannelIds }`.
close_reason: Slack Socket Mode adapter fully implemented and running

title: Skills system — auto-load + agent-written skills
description:
Three sub-tasks:
- Auto-load all skills from ~/.claudeclaw/workspace/skills/ as system prompt context on every run (list skill names + descriptions so Claude knows what's available)
- Establish convention: Claude writes new skills to skills/ during runs with frontmatter `created_by: agent`
- Add `claudeclaw skills` CLI command to list installed skills (name, description, created_by)

title: memory_search and memory_get MCP tools
description: Implement memory_search and memory_get as a stdio MCP server (src/mcp/memory-server.ts). Expose via --mcp-config in runner.ts. Use section-based chunking and keyword scoring for search. Replace the whole-file system prompt injection with tool-based recall.

title: Webhook job triggers
description:
Allow jobs to be triggered via HTTP webhook instead of (or in addition to) cron schedule.
- Add `trigger: webhook` frontmatter option in job .md files
- Add POST /webhook/:job-name endpoint to the existing web server (src/ui/server.ts)
- Fire the job immediately when the endpoint is hit
- Optional: shared secret header for auth

title: VM deployment: headless browser sessions
description: Ensure Playwright works headless on VM. (1) Install chromium + deps (apt-get install chromium-browser libatk1.0-0 libcups2 libxcomposite1 libxrandr2 or npx playwright install chromium --with-deps). (2) Verify --headless flag in browser-mcp.json works without display server. (3) For sites detecting headless: add xvfb fallback (xvfb-run claudeclaw start). (4) Persistent browser sessions: add userDataDir support to Playwright MCP config for cookie/login persistence across invocations. (5) Test LightPanda as lighter alternative on VM.

title: Phase 3.2: Daily engagement brief job
description: New job ~/.claudeclaw/agents/engage/jobs/engagement-brief.md. Runs 9am daily. Consolidates previous day's 3 scout scans + morning fresh scan. Deduplicates, re-ranks by overnight activity. Cross-references with morning brief ideas (matching threads get 5-point bonus). Calls ThreadHQ for top 3 Reddit threads. Output: top 5 engagement actions with draft replies and time estimates. Total daily budget: 20 minutes.

title: Phase 1.1: Auto-draft cron job
description: New job ~/.claudeclaw/agents/content/jobs/auto-draft.md. Runs 7:30am daily. Takes #1 idea from morning brief, generates skeleton draft using voice-corpus.md + 3 Ds framework + recent essays for tone. Writes to content/drafts/YYYY-MM-DD-draft-slug.md with frontmatter (idea_source, platforms, estimated_edit_time, status:skeleton). Cost: 1 sub turn/day.
close_reason: Created auto-draft.md job — runs 7:30am daily, picks idea #1 from morning brief, generates voice-matched skeleton draft with frontmatter

title: VM deployment: systemd service + CLAUDECLAW_HOME env var
description: Make claudeclaw deployable on a VM for always-on operation. (1) Support CLAUDECLAW_HOME env var in src/paths.ts instead of hardcoded ~/.claudeclaw/. (2) Create systemd unit file (Type=simple, Restart=on-failure, RestartSec=10, After=network-online.target). (3) Log to stdout (let systemd journal handle rotation) instead of daemon.log. (4) Add /health HTTP endpoint even when web UI is off — returns JSON with daemon uptime, last heartbeat, last job run, Slack connection status. (5) Document Claude CLI auth requirement (interactive browser login, session persists but needs periodic re-auth). (6) Remove PID file management when running under systemd (systemd tracks PIDs natively).

title: Phase 2.3: Substack automation via StackSweller
description: Blocked on StackSweller chrome extension shipping. When custom Substack Notes API is ready: add substack-publish step to /publish command. Uses StackSweller endpoint to schedule the Substack Note (promotional artifact, not the full essay). DO NOT build separate solution.

title: Memory grooming — heartbeat prompt + auto-compact
description:
Three sub-tasks:
- Update heartbeat prompt (prompts/heartbeat/HEARTBEAT.md) to instruct Claude to update ~/.claudeclaw/workspace/MEMORY.md when it learns new preferences, facts, or patterns about the user
- Add `claudeclaw memory` CLI command to view/edit MEMORY.md in $EDITOR
- Auto-compact MEMORY.md when it exceeds ~4000 tokens: summarize oldest entries, keep recent ones verbatim
close_reason: Implemented all three parts: heartbeat prompt updated, memory CLI command added, auto-compact logic in runner.ts

title: Phase 3.1: Scout job upgrade — scoring + ranking
description: Modify ~/.claudeclaw/agents/engage/jobs/scout.md. Score each thread on 4 dimensions: Relevance (0-10), Timing (0-10), Audience (0-10), Leverage (0-10). Only surface threads scoring 25+/40. Include: link, score breakdown, suggested angle, draft reply, platform tag. Top 5 max per scan.

title: Phase 4.3: Platform analytics collection
description: Future: collect engagement data from all platforms. X via x-intel (ready). Reddit via ThreadHQ engagement history (ready). Substack: scrape dashboard (blocked on StackSweller). LinkedIn: no API, needs browser scraping. Medium: stats page scraping via Playwright. For now, X + Reddit are automated; Substack/LinkedIn/Medium are manual inputs to weekly review.

title: Browser control via Playwright MCP
description:
Wire @playwright/mcp into daemon startup. Steps:
- Add @playwright/mcp as a dependency
- Generate ~/.claudeclaw/mcp.json on daemon start pointing to the playwright MCP server
- Pass `--mcp-config ~/.claudeclaw/mcp.json` to every `claude -p` invocation in runner.ts
- Add `browser` security level or document which existing levels permit browser tools
- Add `playwright` to allowedTools when browser is enabled in settings
close_reason: Playwright MCP integrated and wired into daemon startup
